skill.name: revops
skill.description: Design revenue operations workflows for lead lifecycle stages, lead scoring, routing, CRM automation, pipeline hygiene, and revenue reporting.
skill.summary: Build practical RevOps systems for scoring, routing, handoffs, CRM workflows, and pipeline reporting.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly false positives from RevOps terms, markdown diagrams, and documentation links. No malicious intent, prompt injection, executable code, credential access, or data exfiltration was found. The only real concern is low-risk local context file reading for product marketing context.

low_findings.title: Local Product Marketing Context Read
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE, low severity. The skill asks the agent to read named local context files if they exist. This can expose workspace business context to the active AI session, but the access is limited to expected project context files and does not include exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly says to read local product marketing context files. The risk is bounded because the paths are fixed and no network transfer or secret harvesting is requested.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain RevOps business terms, lifecycle stages, scoring guidance, HubSpot references, or source attribution. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, cryptographic APIs, hashing code, or encryption configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are natural-language sales operations content and evaluation assertions. There is no executable code or cryptographic library usage at these locations.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Static Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The Ruby or shell backtick alerts match markdown code fences and inline file-path formatting. The content describes routing diagrams and context-file checks, not command execution or shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain fenced diagrams or inline markdown references. No Ruby syntax, shell command, subprocess call, or user-controlled command string is present.

low_findings.title: Scheduling And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Business Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The scheduling alerts are CRM workflow schedules such as daily summaries, not operating system at commands. The reconnaissance alerts are ordinary business words in RevOps tables and do not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe CRM schedules, calendar notice periods, lead scoring attributes, and market segments. No host reconnaissance command or OS scheduler usage is present.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Use Parent Paths
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE pattern, low severity. The skill links to a tools registry and integration guides using parent-directory paths. These are static documentation links, not file reads based on user input, but they do reference files outside the skill directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent path sequences are visible in markdown links. The context is documentation navigation, so traversal impact is low and no arbitrary path construction exists.

content.value_statement: Revenue teams lose pipeline when lifecycle rules, routing, and handoffs are unclear. This skill designs practical RevOps systems for scoring, routing, CRM automation, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Defines lifecycle stages from subscriber through customer and evangelist.
Builds lead scoring models using fit, engagement, product usage, and negative signals.
Designs routing rules for territory, account-based, round-robin, and speed-to-lead workflows.
Creates CRM automation playbooks for HubSpot, Salesforce, scheduling tools, and Zapier-style workflows.
Specifies pipeline hygiene rules, deal desk approvals, and dashboard metrics for revenue teams.
limitations: It does not write cold outreach copy or full email nurture sequences.
It needs CRM, GTM motion, ACV, and sales process context for precise recommendations.
It provides implementation specifications and does not change CRM settings by itself.
Benchmarks and thresholds are starting points that teams should calibrate with their own data.

use_cases.title: Create Shared Lifecycle Definitions
use_cases.description: Define MQL, SQL, opportunity, customer, and recycling rules so marketing and sales use the same criteria.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Leads For Sales
use_cases.description: Build a scoring model that combines ICP fit, engagement, product usage, and disqualification signals.

use_cases.title: Improve Pipeline Hygiene
use_cases.description: Set stage exit criteria, stale deal alerts, required fields, and dashboard metrics for cleaner forecasting.

prompt_templates.title: Define Our Lead Lifecycle
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for shared lifecycle stages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help us define our lead lifecycle. We are a B2B SaaS company using [CRM], with [GTM motion], [ACV], and [sales cycle].

prompt_templates.title: Build A Lead Scoring Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a lead scoring model for our [business type]. Include fit, engagement, negative scoring, MQL threshold, and calibration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Lead Routing Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for routing logic and SLAs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design routing rules for leads by territory, company size, named accounts, rep availability, and speed-to-lead SLA.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Our RevOps System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete operating system review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our current RevOps process. Review lifecycle definitions, scoring, routing, CRM automation, pipeline stages, reporting, and handoff risks.

output_examples.input: We need a clear MQL-to-SQL handoff for HubSpot.
output_examples.output: A lifecycle handoff document with entry criteria, owner changes, response SLA, rejection reasons, recycling workflow, and HubSpot workflow steps.

output_examples.input: Our reps work leads unevenly and enterprise accounts need faster routing.
output_examples.output: A routing plan that prioritizes named accounts, enterprise fit, territory ownership, round-robin fallback, and escalation when SLAs are missed.
A dashboard specification with funnel conversion, pipeline velocity, coverage ratio, CAC, LTV:CAC, win rate, and audience-specific views.

best_practices: Provide CRM, GTM motion, ACV, sales cycle, current lifecycle rules, and pain points before asking for recommendations.
Calibrate scoring thresholds with closed-won and closed-lost data before automating MQL promotion.
Review routing, handoff SLAs, and pipeline hygiene with marketing and sales before changing workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not automate lifecycle stages before teams agree on definitions and owners.
Do not use lead scores without negative scoring, decay, and regular recalibration.
Do not route every lead through the same rule when named accounts or enterprise deals need priority.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design RevOps processes for lead lifecycle stages, scoring, routing, CRM workflows, pipeline hygiene, and metrics.

faq.question: Which CRM tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for HubSpot, Salesforce, scheduling tools, enrichment tools, Slack, and Zapier-style workflow automation.

faq.question: Can it build a lead scoring model?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose fit, engagement, product usage, negative scoring, thresholds, and calibration steps.

faq.question: Can it write sales emails?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on RevOps systems and handoffs, not cold outreach copy or email campaign writing.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Share your CRM, GTM motion, ACV, sales cycle, lead volume, current definitions, and where handoffs fail.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found low risk only, mainly local context-file reading and documentation links with parent paths.
